description: Graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor. The top half is a darkened photograph of Donald Trump standing in an ornate interior with gilded arches and chandeliers, wearing a dark navy suit, white shirt, and red tie with an American-flag lapel pin, looking at the camera. Overlaid on the photo in large white serif type is a heading: 'Pell Grants for' (italic) above 'TRADE SCHOOLS!' (bold all-caps). The lower half is a white rounded panel with a small green logo reading 'THE ANNIE E. CASEY FOUNDATION' and two paragraphs of black serif body text: 'Pell Grants are the nation's primary federal financial aid program for students from low-income backgrounds. Unlike student loans, these grants do not need to be repaid. Traditionally, Pell Grants have been available only to students enrolled in longer-term degree or certificate programs. New legislation, passed by Congress in summer 2025, expands Pell Grant eligibility to include shorter-term training programs. Beginning July 1, 2026, eligible learners will be able to use funds for programs that lead directly to good jobs - particularly in the fields such as information technology, health care and skilled trades.'
notable_text: Pell Grants for TRADE SCHOOLS! | New legislation, passed by Congress in summer 2025, expands Pell Grant eligibility to include shorter-term training programs.
